item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was great! Really made me feel bad for the poor guy by the end of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There were a couple really solid SCPs that came out yesterday, and this was definitely one of them. It reminded me a lot of the one that's contained entirely by D-Class personnel, without feeling like any specific parts were lifted directly from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm always a fan of these "secret" SCPs which the O-5 isn't supposed to know about. It is very easy to mess this concept up, but you executed it well. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An SCP that deals with heirarchy, structure, infohazards, and an unqualified gatekeeper of destruction.

This tale went through several iterations but focused on three concepts:

  • An infohazard so dangerous that it would be cataclysmic for any person qualified to deal with it to actually deal with it
  • How to contain an infoahazard without arousing suspicion in anyone at all
  • How a "normal person" would handle doing the toughest part of working for the foundation: killing the innocent because the alternative is far worse.

Thank you to ghostchibi and corpseofbixby for feedback and crit!

Addenda: I apparently forgot to thank cybersqyd for crit as well. I apologize deeply for this oversight as it was unintended.

Image Attribution: "Leather ball MET 19.3.10-Elnaggar", CC0, Wikimedia
